facts.text: Tierra Verde’s name likely honors both the Green brothers and the area’s lush, green land.
The community borders Fort De Soto Park, a 1,136‑acre county facility that spans five islands with historic fortifications.
Archaeologists have found a large burial mound built by the Safety Harbor culture around 1500 AD near the current town site.
Tierra Verde sits on an island just outside Tampa Bay’s mouth, linked to the mainland by the Pinellas Bayway bridges.
Real‑estate development began in 1884 when Hamilton Disston, once the nation’s largest landowner, launched a failed Disston City project that later became Gulfport.
For centuries, the islands that now host Tierra Verde were considered sacred ground by Native peoples, with conflicts erupting when outsiders crossed the boundary.
